name: Message Listing & Show
theme: Read email from the terminal — list messages with filters and show individual messages.

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages list' command exists and runs without error when given mocked AppleScript output representing multiple messages
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages list' via CliRunner with mock_osascript returning canned output for 3+ messages
Verify exit code is 0
Verify output contains message subjects from the canned data
criteria.threshold: Command succeeds with exit code 0 and message data visible in output

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages list' displays a Rich table with columns for: date, from (sender), subject, read/unread status, flagged status, and message ID
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages list' with mocked message data including all six fields
Verify all six data points are visible in the output for each message
Verify message IDs are present (needed for 'messages show' and later commands)
criteria.threshold: Table shows date, from, subject, read/unread, flagged, and message ID per message

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages list' defaults to listing messages from the INBOX mailbox when no --mailbox is specified, and '--mailbox <name>' targets a specific mailbox
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages list' without --mailbox and inspect the AppleScript to verify it targets INBOX
Run 'mailctl messages list --mailbox Sent' and inspect the AppleScript to verify it targets 'Sent'
Verify both invocations succeed with exit code 0
criteria.threshold: Default is INBOX; --mailbox overrides the target mailbox in the generated AppleScript

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages list --account <name>' scopes the listing to a specific account
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages list --account Work' with mock returning messages
Inspect the AppleScript to verify it targets the 'Work' account
Verify the command succeeds with exit code 0
criteria.threshold: AppleScript targets the specified account; command succeeds

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages list --unread' filters to show only unread messages
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages list --unread --json' with mocked data containing both read and unread messages
Parse JSON output and verify only unread messages are present
Alternatively, verify the AppleScript or post-fetch filter includes read status == false condition
criteria.threshold: Only unread messages appear in the output when --unread is specified

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages list --from <sender>' filters messages by sender (case-insensitive substring match)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages list --from alice --json' with mocked data containing messages from alice@example.com and bob@example.com
Parse JSON output and verify only messages from alice appear
Verify the filter is case-insensitive (e.g. '--from Alice' also matches)
criteria.threshold: Only messages matching the sender filter appear in output

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages list --subject <text>' filters messages by subject (case-insensitive substring match)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages list --subject invoice --json' with mocked data containing messages with various subjects
Parse JSON output and verify only messages with 'invoice' in the subject appear
Verify the filter is case-insensitive
criteria.threshold: Only messages matching the subject filter appear in output

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages list --since <date> --before <date>' filters messages by date range
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages list --since 2025-01-01 --before 2025-02-01 --json' with mocked data spanning multiple months
Parse JSON output and verify only messages within the date range appear
Verify --since alone works (no upper bound), and --before alone works (no lower bound)
criteria.threshold: Date range filters correctly include/exclude messages by date

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages list --limit N' caps the number of results returned, with a default of 25
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages list --limit 2 --json' with mocked data containing 5 messages
Verify JSON output contains at most 2 messages
Verify that without --limit, the default cap is 25 (inspect code or test with >25 messages)
criteria.threshold: --limit caps output; default limit is 25

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages list' returns messages sorted by date descending (newest first)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages list --json' with mocked data containing messages with different dates
Parse JSON output and verify dates are in descending order (newest message first)
criteria.threshold: Messages ordered newest-first in the output

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages list --json' outputs valid JSON array with objects containing id, date, from, subject, read, flagged fields
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages list --json' via CliRunner with mocked data
Parse stdout as JSON
Verify it is a list of objects
Verify each object has id, date, from, subject, read (boolean), and flagged (boolean) fields
criteria.threshold: Valid JSON array with all required fields per message

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages list' batches message metadata fetching into a single osascript call (not one per message)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages list' with mock_osascript returning data for multiple messages
Inspect mock_osascript.calls to count invocations
Verify exactly one osascript call was made
criteria.threshold: Exactly one osascript call for the message list operation

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages show <message-id>' command exists and displays a single message with: date, from, to, cc, bcc, subject, and body text
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages show 12345' via CliRunner with mock_osascript returning a full message
Verify exit code is 0
Verify output contains the from address, to address, subject, and body text
Verify cc and bcc fields are shown when present
criteria.threshold: Show command displays all core message fields

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages show <id>' displays attachment metadata (filename, size, MIME type) when the message has attachments
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages show <id>' with mock returning a message with two attachments
Verify output includes attachment filenames
Verify output includes attachment sizes
Verify output includes MIME types
criteria.threshold: Attachment metadata (name, size, MIME type) visible in output

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages show <id> --headers' displays all message headers
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages show <id> --headers' with mock returning a message with headers
Verify output includes header fields like Message-ID, Date, From, To, Subject, etc.
Verify the headers section is distinct from the body
criteria.threshold: All headers displayed when --headers flag is used

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages show <id> --raw' displays the unprocessed message body without formatting
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages show <id> --raw' with mock returning a message with HTML body
Verify the raw body content is output without Rich formatting or HTML stripping
Verify the output differs from the default (formatted) output
criteria.threshold: Raw body output without formatting when --raw is specified

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages show <id> --json' outputs valid JSON with all message fields including id, date, from, to, cc, bcc, subject, body, attachments, read, flagged
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages show <id> --json' with mock returning a full message
Parse stdout as JSON
Verify the object has id, date, from, to, cc, bcc, subject, body, attachments, read, flagged fields
Verify attachments is a list of objects with name, size, and mime_type
criteria.threshold: Valid JSON object with all message and attachment fields

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages show' accepts a message-id as a positional argument (not a flag), matching the ID format output by 'messages list'
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages show <id>' where <id> matches the format used in messages list output
Verify the command passes the correct ID to the AppleScript query
Verify the command fails with a usage error when no ID is provided
criteria.threshold: Message ID is a positional argument; no-ID invocation produces usage error

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages show <nonexistent-id>' produces a clear error message when the message ID is not found
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages show NONEXISTENT' with mock returning an AppleScript error indicating message not found
Verify exit code is non-zero
Verify error message indicates the message was not found
criteria.threshold: Non-zero exit code and error message for missing message ID

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages list' produces a clear error when Mail.app is not running
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages list' with mock_osascript configured to simulate Mail.app not running
Verify exit code is non-zero
Verify error message mentions Mail.app
criteria.threshold: Non-zero exit code and user-friendly error message on stderr

criteria.description: Messages commands send data to stdout and errors to stderr, following the convention established in Sprint 2
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages list' successfully and verify data on stdout
Run 'mailctl messages list' with an error and verify error message on stderr
Run 'mailctl messages show <id>' successfully and verify data on stdout
criteria.threshold: Stdout for data, stderr for errors, consistent with Sprint 2 pattern

criteria.description: Unit tests exist for 'messages list' covering: success with multiple messages, each filter option (--unread, --from, --subject, --since, --before, --limit), --json output, default INBOX, and batch call assertion
testSteps: Run pytest for the messages test module
Verify tests exist covering: multi-message success, --unread filter, --from filter, --subject filter, date filters, --limit, --json, default mailbox, batch call count
Verify all tests pass
criteria.threshold: At least 10 distinct test cases for messages list, all passing

criteria.description: Unit tests exist for 'messages show' covering: full message display, attachments metadata, --headers flag, --raw flag, --json output, and message-not-found error
testSteps: Run pytest for the messages show test module
Verify tests exist covering: full message display, attachments, --headers, --raw, --json, error case
Verify all tests pass
criteria.threshold: At least 6 distinct test cases for messages show, all passing

criteria.description: Full test suite (pytest from project root) passes with zero failures, including all Sprint 1, Sprint 2, and Sprint 3 tests
testSteps: Run 'pytest -v' from the project root
Verify all tests pass (0 failures, 0 errors)
Verify Sprint 1 engine tests and Sprint 2 accounts/mailboxes tests still pass (regression check)
criteria.threshold: All tests pass, exit code 0

criteria.description: Messages commands are in their own module (e.g., src/mailctl/commands/messages.py) following the same architecture as accounts/mailboxes: separate script generation, parsing, fetching, and Typer registration
testSteps: Verify messages command logic is in its own module under src/mailctl/commands/
Verify AppleScript generation is in dedicated build_*_script() functions
Verify output parsing is in dedicated parse_*_output() functions
Verify the module uses the shared render_output() and handle_mail_error() from the output module
criteria.threshold: Messages module follows the established architecture pattern with clean layering

criteria.description: 'mailctl --version', 'mailctl --help', 'mailctl accounts list', and 'mailctl mailboxes list' all still work correctly (Sprint 1 & 2 functionality preserved)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl --version' and verify it prints version and exits 0
Run 'mailctl --help' and verify it lists accounts, mailboxes, and messages as command groups
Run existing Sprint 1 and Sprint 2 unit tests and verify they still pass
criteria.threshold: All Sprint 1 and Sprint 2 functionality still works; messages group visible in help

criteria.description: Multiple filter options can be combined (e.g., '--unread --from alice --since 2025-01-01 --limit 10') and all are applied together
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages list --unread --from alice --limit 5 --json' with mocked data containing mixed messages
Parse JSON output and verify only messages matching ALL filters appear
Verify the result count respects --limit
criteria.threshold: All specified filters are applied conjunctively

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages --help' lists available subcommands including 'list' and 'show', and 'mailctl messages list --help' describes all filter options
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages --help' and verify it mentions 'list' and 'show' subcommands
Run 'mailctl messages list --help' and verify it describes --mailbox, --account, --unread, --from, --subject, --since, --before, --limit, and --json options
Run 'mailctl messages show --help' and verify it describes the message-id argument and --headers, --raw, --json options
criteria.threshold: All help outputs are informative and mention key options

outOfScope: HTML-to-text conversion sophistication — basic stripping is sufficient for this sprint
Attachment downloading or content display — metadata only (filename, size, MIME type)
Full RFC-2822 header parsing — basic header display is sufficient
Paginated output or scrolling for long message bodies
